_comment: Connector-specific edits to GENERATED files that a cli-printing-press reprint must NOT clobber. check_handfixes.py asserts every marker below is still present (and banned anti-patterns absent) and fails CI if a reprint dropped one. Provenance: project_mcp_sync_envvar_and_promote_toolsmanifest.md (reprint 2026-06-06, press 4.22.0, Level RMM).

handfixes.summary: Auth env var stays the spec canonical LEVEL_API_TOKEN everywhere; mcp-sync/promote rewrite it to the slug-derived LEVELIO_API_TOKEN, breaking MCP auth
handfixes.why: Level's spec x-auth-env-vars is LEVEL_API_TOKEN (not LEVELIO_*). config.go reads LEVEL_API_TOKEN, but press 4.22.0 `mcp-sync` derives the var from the api_name slug and rewrites manifest.json (user_config + mcp_config.env), .printing-press.json, tools.go agent hints, and tools-manifest.json to LEVELIO_API_TOKEN while config.go keeps the canonical - a silent 401 for every .mcpb/MCP user. `lock promote` re-triggers it. A reprint/polish/promote plants the bug again. The fix keeps LEVEL_API_TOKEN aligned across config.go + manifest + server + tools.go.
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Fix belongs in the press: mcp-sync must honor x-auth-env-vars and never derive the auth var from the api_name slug. Filed as a retro (manuscripts/levelio/20260606-173908 findings).
